title: Traveling with Kyle - Brazil Part 3 - Rio, Carneval

transcript.text: There was some like boogie boarding there, or body boarding, which was really fun.
The waves were huge, and they were totally jam-packed with people,
But I was having fun being in there with millions of other people
And riding the waves with my body on these crowded beaches.
And the subway system was really good.
I could just take the subway right down there, and it wasn't too expensive.
And I met this American guy, older American guy, who's like 60 or something,
Who I guess used to work for the FBI, but he decided to become an English teacher working for himself in Rio.
And he told me of a really good hotel to stay in in Rio, in the downtown area,
Right at the base of these really famous steps.
And if you ever go there, that's the place you want to go, because it's the cheapest and it's the best location.
These really famous steps that are all colored.
Every color is a different color, and it goes to the bottom.
But, and I guess he told me that that's where all of the, or right at the top of the stairs,
That's like where the Flavela started, and that's where all the, like, the muggers and stuff lived.
And he said the week before I had gotten there, there was a dead body.
There was some guy who had been killed who was there, like, right at the base of the steps.
But it was a cool place to stay because every, every Friday night they have these big parties,
These outdoor parties right in front of it.
And so I hung out with him for a couple days, and he wanted to take me.
He had this fun thing that he did where you, like, climb to the top of that stone thing that the gondola goes to.
But it sounded like it was, like, on a sheer cliff, so it was, like, crazy.
I was like, I don't think so.
I'm kind of getting ready to go.
But, yeah, I just kind of hung out there, saw all the, like, the museums and the scenes and stuff.
I went to a jazz bar one night, actually when I first got there,
And I met some kid who I actually went to junior high with.
And we went there with him and his French friend, and we watched the jazz people play.
It was really good.
And then I was hanging out with one of the jazz performers, and he was like,
Yeah, you know, I can get some weed.
And he actually hooked me up.
I went to his apartment and hooked me up with a big old bag of weed.
So I was stoned off my ass the whole time I was in, you know, just as I was the whole time I was in one of those high rates.
And he wanted to hang out with me for a carnival, but for some reason I didn't call him.
And I did take the, what's it called, the little subway thing, the little cable car,
All the way to the top of where the Jesus Christ is.
It's this huge statue that's like, I don't know how high it is, a couple hundred feet high,
Really big, holding his arms out like this that you can see.
And from a long ways away, only problem is there was, it was on a really foggy day that I went up there,
And I could only see parts of the downtown.
I couldn't see all around.
And I didn't go up there again.
I should have bought a bicycle, just like a really cheap street bike,
And ridden my bike up there, because there was a, there was like a road that you could go up there on a car.
I could have ridden my bike all over the place.
I took the, a little cable car up past an interesting, like, kind of like bohemian artist center place.
It was right above where the downtown area is.
And I walked into the flabella, and I was going to like walk all over the flabella.
Um, and I actually walked in a little bit of it, and it was really basic, like, like, you know,
The main streets through the neighborhood are just like six feet wide, totally unpaved dirt paths.
And just these houses built out of like cardboard and toothpicks, you know.
And you could see the flabellas up on the sides of the hills, like built really, really high.
From right next to like the downtown area, and it would have been cool to, oh, 420.
It would have been really cool to, uh, do the tour.
I guess they had paid tours.
I should have done that, but yeah, I ended up not walking like really far in, because I was like,
Oh man, I don't want to get, like, jumped or anything.
So, uh, yeah, I came.
What else did I do?
Um, and then I bumped into my French friend, who I, uh, did the boat tour with down in Patagonia
And Chile before, and hung out with him for a few days also in Buenos Aires.
And, uh, we went down to the beach one day, and then there was this like disco there, and
What is it, Copacabana Beach.
There's this disco there, I can't remember what it's called, but you go in, and it's loaded
With prostitutes dancing.
But they're all really hot, really young, really hot, and they're all dressed really
Sexy, like, in their own theme.
Like, there are these two girls dressed like Catholic schoolgirls, who this old, like, 60-year-old
German guy was with.
And my French friend, who's, like, really, like, outgoing, and, like, he's, like, a player
Type of a guy, was talking to one of them, and the German guy got really pissed off at
Him, and was like, you're messing with my girlfriend, she's my girlfriend.
But he ended up cooling down, and I guess, kind of becoming friends with us after that,
But it's funny.
But, like, these girls would, like, they'd come up and they'd dance right in front of you,
And then they'd want you to buy them a beer, and then, like, another beer, and I guess
If you don't buy them, like, if you buy them one beer, I guess, then you have to buy them
Another beer right after, and if you don't, they'll just get somebody else to buy them
A beer, and then I guess they'll want to take you home afterwards, but.
And I talked to some tourists there, other American tourists, and, like, one guy, he was
Mugged right on the beach in front of everybody, so I guess I got lucky that I didn't get mugged.
And some other guy was actually drugged.
He went back and was hanging out in this hotel room with some Brazilian guys, and next thing
He knows, and I guess they made him a tea, and he was drinking, and the next thing he
Knows, he wakes up from, like, a comatose state, and he had, like, all of his money and
Everything was gone, and so you'd have to be careful there.
And, yeah, I did take a taxi back one night, and I gave the guy, you know, money, and he
Didn't give me his, he didn't want to give me change, so you definitely want to be careful.
But, uh, yeah, that disco was definitely an interesting place.
And, uh, yeah, so, I was kind of wandering around, basically, over there.
Then the, uh, the, uh, that was interesting.
They just have, down on Copacabana Beach, all the way down to Ipanema Beach, they have
These huge parades of people, um, jam-packed just walking down, there's lots of different
Floats, and all the floats, you have all these people dancing, and, like, women dressed up,
And, well, everybody's seen the pictures of Carnival.
And, um, but, yeah, Brazilians, they're really kind of laid back, like, people would just be
Peeing right there on the side of the road, and not really, you know, worry about getting
Busted.
Um, I got pickpocketed, though, like, you wouldn't believe.
Like, I bought a little rainbow hat and put it in my back pocket, and then,
I went to get it 30 seconds later, and it was already gone.
But, uh, yeah, so I, you know, I wasn't stupid.
I, I had my money on my money belt.
But I still got pickpocketed with cash I had in my pocket, and it was just, um, but then I
Got a, there was, yeah, the place I was, that was really cool, because all throughout, uh,
Carnival, right in downtown area, they had these outdoor, uh, outdoor, uh, concerts.
And all night long, until the morning, they just had different band after different band
Playing right there.
It's really cool.
So, and, uh, yeah, and it's, man, Brazil is definitely also a great place if you want
To, if you want to be like a chick magnet.
I was on the beach one day, and, and, uh, there was this, uh, really cute black girl,
And I just, like, looked at her.
I was kind of looking at her.
And then she, like, noticed, so then she said her, like, motion to her friend, who was
This hot Latin girl, who just ran right up to me and sat in my lap and started making
Out with me.
And, uh, she wanted, and then she's like, where's your hotel?
Let's go to your hotel.
And I, and I said, I said something like, she, oh, yeah, no, no, she wanted to go do
Something and meet me at six o'clock.
But I thought six o'clock was in, like, two hours.
So I was like, ah, no, but I realized that it was in 10 minutes.
So, kind of blew that one.
But, uh, but then I had this other, like, really hot Latin girlfriend who I met on the
Party that was right in front of my hotel room.
And my, uh, but, yeah, anyways, um, yeah, that's what, that's, I mean, I also experienced
That kind of a thing in, like, Salvador.
Like, all you do is, like, look at these, especially these black girls, and then they just,
Like, run right up to you and ask you where your hotel room is.
But, uh, anyways, whoops, I'm out of time.
